plan: Introduce myself as `mei_kovacs_8020` and provide zip code `28236` when the agent asks for account/identity verification.
State the intent to exchange two items: the water bottle (reason: want a bigger size) and the desk lamp (reason: want a brighter one).
When asked about the desk lamp replacement preference, specify brightness as the priority and state power-source preference order: AC adapter first, then battery, then USB.
If the agent presents options for either item, evaluate the water bottle options for larger size and the desk lamp options for higher brightness and preferred power source.

If the agent asks for confirmation of the exchanges (either individually or together), **only confirm the desk lamp exchange** — do not confirm or proceed with the water bottle exchange at that point.
Complete and finalize only the desk lamp exchange as the confirmed transaction.

trajectory.thought: I need to start a conversation with the customer service agent, introducing myself as mei_kovacs_8020. Since I need to use the available tools to interact with the agent, let me think about what to do here.

My power source preference order is: AC adapter first (preferred), then battery-powered, and USB as the last resort. I will clearly communicate that brightness is the top priority in selecting the replacement lamp.

This step is about evaluating options if/when the customer service agent presents replacement choices. The evaluation criteria are: for the water bottle, select the option with larger capacity/size; for the desk lamp, select the option with higher brightness (lumens), and among equally bright options, prefer AC adapter power source over battery over USB. At this step, if the customer service agent asks me to confirm either or both exchanges, I will only confirm the desk lamp exchange. I will explicitly decline or not confirm the water bottle exchange. This means if asked about both together, I respond that I want to proceed with the desk lamp exchange only, not the water bottle. If asked individually, I confirm the desk lamp and decline the water bottle.

All previous steps were thought-only planning steps. The actual execution of the role-play has been captured conceptually.
